headline: Microsoft cuts Xbox Game Pass price, to remove 'Call of Duty' day one inclusions
summary: Microsoft has dropped the price of its video game subscription service and the platform will ‌not include future "Call of Duty" game releases on launch ‌day, the company said on Tuesday, in the first major strategy shift under ​new gaming boss Asha Sharma.  Starting Tuesday, Xbox Game Pass Ultimate will now cost $22.99 a month from $29.99 while PC Game Pass is priced at $13.99 from $16.49 monthly, the software firm said in a blog.  After ‌years of betting that ⁠subscription services and cloud gaming would become the next big driver for the Xbox brand, consistent ⁠Game Pass price hikes, declining console sales and a lack of compelling titles left Microsoft trailing rivals Sony and Nintendo.

raw.headline: Microsoft cuts Xbox Game Pass price, to remove 'Call of Duty' day one inclusions
raw.summary: Microsoft has dropped the price of its video game subscription service and the platform will ‌not include future "Call of Duty" game releases on launch ‌day, the company said on Tuesday, in the first major strategy shift under ​new gaming boss Asha Sharma.  Starting Tuesday, Xbox Game Pass Ultimate will now cost $22.99 a month from $29.99 while PC Game Pass is priced at $13.99 from $16.49 monthly, the software firm said in a blog.  After ‌years of betting that ⁠subscription services and cloud gaming would become the next big driver for the Xbox brand, consistent ⁠Game Pass price hikes, declining console sales and a lack of compelling titles left Microsoft trailing rivals Sony and Nintendo.

headline: Microsoft must face $2.8 billion UK lawsuit over cloud computing licences
summary: Microsoft must face a mass lawsuit alleging it overcharged thousands of British businesses to use Windows Server software ‌on cloud computing services provided by Amazon, Google and Alibaba, ‌a London tribunal ruled on Tuesday.  Competition lawyer Maria Luisa Stasi is bringing the case on behalf ​of nearly 60,000 businesses that run Windows Server on rival cloud platforms.  They argued at a hearing last year that the businesses were overcharged because ‌Microsoft charges higher wholesale ⁠prices for Windows Server than for users of Azure, costs that are passed on to customers and make Azure cheaper ⁠than Amazon's AWS or Google Cloud.

raw.headline: Microsoft must face $2.8 billion UK lawsuit over cloud computing licences
raw.summary: Microsoft must face a mass lawsuit alleging it overcharged thousands of British businesses to use Windows Server software ‌on cloud computing services provided by Amazon, Google and Alibaba, ‌a London tribunal ruled on Tuesday.  Competition lawyer Maria Luisa Stasi is bringing the case on behalf ​of nearly 60,000 businesses that run Windows Server on rival cloud platforms.  They argued at a hearing last year that the businesses were overcharged because ‌Microsoft charges higher wholesale ⁠prices for Windows Server than for users of Azure, costs that are passed on to customers and make Azure cheaper ⁠than Amazon's AWS or Google Cloud.

headline: Apple's new CEO is a product perfectionist taking on the AI age
summary: To understand how Apple's new CEO John Ternus will run the company, pay attention to what he refuses to sell.  "We never think about shipping a technology," Ternus, 50, said in a recent interview about AI ‌with tech review site Tom's Guide.  "We always think about how can we leverage technology to ship amazing products."

headline: Adobe CX Enterprise Pushes AI Agents Deeper Into Customer Experience Budgets
summary: Adobe, ticker NasdaqGS:ADBE, announced CX Enterprise, an agentic AI platform aimed at large businesses. The suite introduces business-focused AI agents for customer experience, digital marketing, content production, governance, and sales. Adobe is collaborating with Amazon, Microsoft, Anthropic, OpenAI, and NVIDIA to support CX Enterprise across enterprise technology stacks.
